# Daniel Kalman — Portfolio

> Design engineer with 10+ years of experience. Bridges design and frontend engineering — designs in Figma, builds in React and Svelte, thinks in systems. Currently at Entropy (blockchain workflow automation platform, since June 2025). Previously: Radicle, Parity Technologies, Linkurious, DotLoop, Prezi, SatoshiPay.

Philosophy: "Designer who codes. Strong design foundations matter more than ever when working with AI. I build prototypes to validate ideas fast and design systems to keep things from falling apart."

Open to new opportunities and collaborations.

---

## Case Studies

### Good Listener (https://dnlklmn.online/good-listener) — 2026
Local AI transcription app for therapists. Built entirely by Daniel: product strategy, UX research, UI design, Tauri/Svelte frontend. Privacy-first — runs entirely locally using Whisper models, no cloud uploads.

The problem: psychology practices are buried in admin. The "transcribe-anonymise-summarise-write report" pipeline repeats at every session, eating the time therapists most need for patients.

Process: 1-hour user interview with a practicing psychologist, pain points framework built with Claude, multiple prototypes (DSM-5 MCP client, n8n/Scriberr transcription workflow, CLI with local models), Langfuse integration for tracing and eval-driven improvement.

Tech: Figma, Tauri, Svelte, Whisper, n8n, MCP servers, Langfuse, Claude API.
Best for: AI product teams, health/privacy-sensitive tools, eval-driven development, full-stack design engineering roles.

---

### Auto (https://dnlklmn.online/auto) — 2025
Visual workflow builder for blockchain automations with real-time multiplayer. Built at Entropy Cryptography. Bridges on-chain triggers and off-chain actions.

Process: parallel research tracks (workflow map structure + crypto use cases), multiple prototypes (directed vs. freeform, atomic vs. use-case nodes), forked n8n for real-world experimentation, then designed with a limited node set to reduce cognitive load. Implemented multiplayer with Yjs CRDTs + WebRTC.

Live demo available at https://dnlklmn.online/auto.

Tech: Figma, Svelte, Yjs, WebRTC.
Best for: Web3/blockchain companies, complex data-heavy apps, collaborative/multiplayer SaaS.

---

### Radicle Desktop (https://dnlklmn.online/radicle-desktop) — 2024
Desktop app for Radicle, a decentralized peer-to-peer code collaboration platform. Manage issues, patches, code review, and notifications. Write and review offline, sync when back online.

Process: per-feature research → hypothesis → wireframes → prototype → high-fidelity handoff, validated with both users and engineering team. Key challenge: making Radicle's peer-to-peer model (peers at different revisions, no central source of truth) legible without hiding what makes it different from GitHub.

Tech: Figma, Svelte.
Best for: Developer tools, open source products, rigorous user research, local-first/offline-first apps.

---

### Radicle Design System (https://dnlklmn.online/radicle-design-system) — 2024
Complete design system for the Radicle ecosystem. Design tokens, component library, Figma integration, CSS variables, icon system — connecting design tooling directly to code.

Process: UI audit → color system + typographic scale + components in Figma → token export pipeline → Svelte implementation. Tokens stored in a shared Radicle repo as single source of truth. Dark and light mode support.

Tech: Figma, Tokens Studio, Style Dictionary, Svelte.
Best for: Companies scaling a design system, design-engineering collaboration, platform teams.

---

## Writing (https://dnlklmn.online/blog)

### Why I design again (https://dnlklmn.online/blog/do-design) — Dec 2025
On the temptation to skip design when AI can ship working software by lunch — and why that's a trap. Design is a decision-making framework. In multi-agent workflows, bad problem framing cascades through every layer. "AI didn't make design obsolete. It made it the highest-leverage thing I can spend time on."

### A design system is documented decisions (https://dnlklmn.online/blog/design-systems-make-vibe-coding-real) — Feb 2026
Design tokens aren't just a component library — they're documented decisions. Without them, each agent prompt reinvents spacing, color, and layout from scratch. With a design system, decisions compound in your favor. "I build the system first. Then I vibe as hard as I want."

---

## AI Interfaces

This portfolio exposes two AI interfaces for agents and LLMs:

### Conversational chat API
Endpoint: https://dnlklmn.online/api/chat
Method: POST
Content-Type: application/json
Body: { "messages": [{ "role": "user", "content": "..." }] }
Returns: { "reply": "..." }

An AI guide (powered by Claude) that understands who you are and recommends relevant case studies. Maintains conversation context across up to 6 exchanges.

### MCP Server
URL: https://dnlklmn.online/api/mcp
Protocol: Model Context Protocol (MCP), StreamableHTTP transport

Tools available:
- get_overview — Daniel's bio, philosophy, availability
- list_case_studies — all case studies with summaries
- get_case_study(slug) — full detail for a specific case study
- list_blog_posts — published blog posts
- get_blog_post(slug) — full post content
- ask_daniel(question) — conversational query answered by Claude

To use in Claude Desktop, add to your config:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "daniel-kalman": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://dnlklmn.online/api/mcp"
    }
  }
}
